metadata.title: Why Only 0.7% of a Cancer Nanoparticle Reaches the Tumor

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Aloha, Liam — in for Bear. And still, only 0.7 percent reaches the tumor? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: And still, only 0.7 percent reaches the tumor?

beats.narration_text: A researcher injects a dose of tumor-targeted nanoparticles into a mouse. Only 0.7 percent reaches the tumor. The other 99.3 percent goes somewhere else.

card.copy: Why Only 0.7% of a Cancer Nanoparticle Reaches the Tumor

beats.narration_text: The particle was designed carefully. It carries a targeting ligand that binds receptors overexpressed on cancer cells. In cell culture it kills at a fraction of the normal dose.

props.headline: The particle was designed carefully. It carries a targeting ligand that…

beats.scene_description: a vial of nanoparticle solution on a laboratory bench, beside a syringe — editorial newsprint collage style, flat desaturated plate, slow push-in on the vial label
beats.image_prompt: B02, glass vial of clear nanoparticle solution and a syringe on a white laboratory bench, one vial and one syringe, horizontal composition, flat overhead light, no shadows, desaturated documentary photograph pinned to aged newsprint like an editorial clipping, no people, no text visible on label
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL-ai] the engineered particle — vial and syringe on bench

beats.narration_text: So why does so little arrive? A tumor-targeted nanoparticle is injected into the bloodstream. The targeting ligand is working. And still, only 0.7 percent reaches the tumor.

card.copy: A tumor-targeted nanoparticle is injected. Only 0.7% reaches the tumor. The targeting ligand is working. So why does so little arrive?

beats.narration_text: Because between the syringe and the tumor cell, the dose is lost at every step. Not one step — five sequential steps. Fail at any of them and the particle delivers nothing.

production_viz.label: five steps from syringe to tumor
production_viz.mechanic: a wide TEAL bar labeled 'Injected Dose' sits left; five crimson step labels appear sequentially to the right, with arrows between them, establishing the chain before the drain begins
production_viz.note: establish the chain structure — no numbers yet

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] five-step chain from injection to tumor cell

beats.narration_text: Step one: survive circulation. The liver and spleen clear foreign particles from the blood within minutes. Most of the dose never gets a second chance.

production_viz.label: step 1 — circulation loss
production_viz.mechanic: the full TEAL dose bar shrinks from the right as a CRIMSON loss chip 'CLEARED BY LIVER / SPLEEN' appears; a TEAL remainder label updates; the 'drain' move — bar clips leftward
production_viz.note: first drain beat; wide bar shrinks to show loss at step 1

beats.narration_text: Step two: escape the vessel. Particles must exit the bloodstream into tumor tissue through leaky vessel walls. Many degrade or get trapped before they can.

production_viz.label: step 2 — extravasation loss
production_viz.mechanic: the surviving TEAL bar from step 1 drains further; CRIMSON chip 'FAILS TO EXTRAVASATE' appears; TEAL remainder bar narrows again
production_viz.note: second drain beat; continuing the funnel

beats.narration_text: Steps three, four, and five: penetrate the dense tumor matrix, get taken up by cells, and release the payload inside. Each step cuts the arriving dose again.

production_viz.label: steps 3-4-5 — penetration, uptake, release
production_viz.mechanic: three more sequential drain moves compress into one beat; three crimson loss chips appear in sequence as the TEAL bar narrows to a thin sliver; GOLD label '0.7%' appears on the sliver at the end
production_viz.note: the funnel resolves to 0.7%; gold highlights the final number

beats.narration_text: Adding a targeting ligand only helps at step four — cell uptake. A particle that fails step one, cleared from circulation in the first hour, never reaches step four.

production_viz.label: targeting only fixes step 4
production_viz.mechanic: the five-step chain from B04 reappears; a GOLD bracket appears under step 4 labeled 'targeting ligand helps here'; steps 1-3 are ringed or marked CRIMSON as 'still lost'; the message is visual — fix late step but early steps still kill
production_viz.note: key implication beat — targeting does not fix early loss

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] targeting only addresses step 4 — early steps dominate loss

beats.narration_text: The particle was not misbehaving. The targeting ligand was binding correctly. The delivery chain failed upstream, long before the ligand had any role.

card.copy: Most of what is injected does not arrive. Adding a targeting ligand to a particle that fails at step one does not solve the problem.

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] quote — targeting cannot fix an early-step failure

beats.narration_text: Take 100 illustrative units. Eighteen are cleared by spleen and liver in hour one. Fourteen more degrade in circulation. Twelve exit the vessel but cannot penetrate the matrix. Fifty-five reach non-tumor tissue. One unit — 0.7 — reaches the tumor and releases payload.

production_viz.label: 100 units tracked — illustrative
production_viz.mechanic: two-column layout: left column CRIMSON header 'lost at each step' with a breakdown (18 + 14 + 12 + 55 = 99.3); right column TEAL header 'reaches tumor' showing 0.7; MONO font for numbers; a small MONO note bar at bottom reads 'numbers illustrative'
production_viz.note: STANDARD EXAMPLE template — illustrative numbers labeled clearly

beats.narration_text: A perfect targeting ligand solves one step of five. The funnel leaks everywhere upstream. Until those steps are measured and fixed, 0.7 percent is the answer.

card.copy: Why only 0.7%? Five steps lose dose before the ligand has a chance.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. Adding a targeting ligand only helps at step four — cell uptake. A particle that fails step one, cleared from circulation in the first hour, never reaches step four. The particle was not misbehaving. The targeting ligand was binding correctly. The delivery chain failed upstream, long before the ligand had any role.

props.artifactHeading: Why Only 0.7% of a Cancer Nanoparticle Reaches
artifactLines: Adding a targeting ligand only helps at step four — cell uptake.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why Only 0.7% of a Cancer Nanoparticle Reaches the Tumor] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?

props.title: Why Only 0.7% of a Cancer Nanoparticle Reaches the Tumor
props.subline: until those steps are measured and fixed, 0.7 percent is the answer
